summary: Brief Viola Lee Blues tease; limited Dead material, mixed performance
blurb: The show consists only of a short instrumental rendition of Viola Lee Blues captured in decent‑quality audio. Reviewers describe it as more of a tease than a full set and note the playing is not by the Grateful Dead. Overall listening value is limited.
review: On October 31, 1966 at California Hall the Grateful Dead’s setlist lists only Viola Lee Blues and Alice D. Millionaire, yet the sole surviving tape captures just a brief instrumental jam of Viola Lee Blues. Listeners report that the source sounds decent – one fan even calls it “5‑star sound” – but the performance itself is not by the Dead, feeling more like an unfinished tease than a proper concert offering. With no full band interplay to evaluate and only a fragment of material present, this recording serves mainly as a historical curiosity rather than a must‑listen show for most Deadheads.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The single analysis reports mixed sentiment; reviewers neither strongly praise nor heavily criticize the performance, leading to a neutral adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Only available source; decent audio quality captures the brief Viola Lee Blues jam despite not being a full Dead performance.

key_highlights: Short instrumental Viola Lee Blues captured in decent audio
Performance not by the Grateful Dead; feels like a tease
Audio quality is good for an SBD source
